  have h_main : Set A ∼ 𝒫 A := by

    have h : 𝒫 A = {s : Set U | s ⊆ A} := by

    -- Use the fact that the set of all subsets of A is equivalent to the powerset of A
    have h₂ : Set A ∼ {s : Set U | s ⊆ A} := by
      -- Define a function that maps each subset of A to itself
      let f : Set A → {s : Set U | s ⊆ A} := fun s => ⟨s.val, by
        -- Prove that each subset of A is a subset of A
        have h₃ : s.val ⊆ A := by
          intro x hx
          exact Set.mem_of_mem s.val hx
        exact h₃⟩
      -- Define the inverse function
      let g : {s : Set U | s ⊆ A} → Set A := fun s => ⟨s.val, by
        -- Prove that each subset of A is a subset of A
        have h₃ : s.val ⊆ A := s.property
        exact h₃⟩
      -- Prove that the functions are inverses of each other

        intro s t h₄
        simp [f] at h₄
        <;> aesop
      have h₄ : Function.Surjective f := by
        intro s
        -- For each subset of A, find a subset of A that maps to it

          have h₅ : s.val ⊆ A := s.property
          exact h₅⟩
        simp [f]
        <;> aesop

        intro s t h₆
        simp [g] at h₆
        <;> aesop
      have h₆ : Function.Surjective g := by
        intro s
        -- For each subset of A, find a subset of A that maps to it

            intro x hx
            exact Set.mem_of_mem s.val hx
          exact h₇⟩
        simp [g]
        <;> aesop
      -- Use the bijection to prove the equivalence
      exact ⟨f, g, by
        -- Prove that f and g are inverses of each other
        constructor <;> ext <;> simp [f, g] <;> aesop⟩
    -- Combine the equivalences to get the final result
    calc
      Set A ∼ {s : Set U | s ⊆ A} := h₂
      _ = 𝒫 A := by
        rw [h]
        <;> simp [Set.ext_iff]
        <;> aesop
  exact h_main

by
  have h : Set A ∼ 𝒫 A := by
    classical
    -- Use the fact that the set of subsets of A is in bijective correspondence with the set of functions from A to Bool
    -- This is a standard result in set theory and can be leveraged here
    have h₁ : Set A ≃ 𝒫 A := by
      apply Set.ext
      intro s
      simp [Set.mem_setOf_eq]
      <;>
      aesop
    -- The above equivalence is actually a bijection, so we can use it to establish the equinumerity
    exact h₁.bijection
  exact h

  have h : Set A ∼ 𝒫 A := by
    classical
    -- Define the bijection between the power set of A and the set of subsets of A
    let f : Set A → 𝒫 A := fun s => Set.range (fun (x : A) => x.val)
    -- Prove that f is a bijection
    have h₁ : Function.Bijective f := by
      -- Prove that f is injective
      have h₂ : Function.Injective f := by
        intro s t hst
        -- If f(s) = f(t), then s = t
        have h₃ : Set.range (fun (x : s) => (x : A).val) = Set.range (fun (x : t) => (x : A).val) := by simpa [f] using hst
        -- Use the fact that the ranges are equal to show that s and t are equal
        have h₄ : s = t := by
          apply Set.Subset.antisymm
          -- Prove that s ⊆ t
          · intro x hx
            have h₅ : (x : A).val ∈ Set.range (fun (x : t) => (x : A).val) := by
              rw [h₃]
              exact Set.mem_range_self ⟨x, hx⟩
            obtain ⟨y, hy⟩ := h₅
            have h₆ : (y : A).val = (x : A).val := by simp_all [Subtype.ext_iff]
            have h₇ : (y : A) = (x : A) := by
              apply Subtype.ext
              simp_all [Subtype.ext_iff]
            have h₈ : y.val ∈ t := by exact y.2
            simp_all [Subtype.ext_iff]
          -- Prove that t ⊆ s
          · intro x hx
            have h₅ : (x : A).val ∈ Set.range (fun (x : s) => (x : A).val) := by
              rw [h₃]
              exact Set.mem_range_self ⟨x, hx⟩
            obtain ⟨y, hy⟩ := h₅
            have h₆ : (y : A).val = (x : A).val := by simp_all [Subtype.ext_iff]
            have h₇ : (y : A) = (x : A) := by
              apply Subtype.ext
              simp_all [Subtype.ext_iff]
            have h₈ : y.val ∈ s := by exact y.2
            simp_all [Subtype.ext_iff]
        exact h₄
      -- Prove that f is surjective
      have h₃ : Function.Surjective f := by
        intro s
        -- For any subset s of A, find a set in Set A that maps to s
        use {x : A | x.val ∈ s}
        ext x
        simp [f]
        <;>
        (try simp_all [Set.mem_setOf_eq, Subtype.mem_setOf_eq]) <;>
        (try aesop) <;>
        (try

              <;> aesop
            }) <;>
            (try {

            })
          })
      -- Combine injectivity and surjectivity to get bijectivity
      exact ⟨h₂, h₃⟩
    -- Use the bijection to prove the equinumerosity
    exact h₁.equinumerous
  -- Use the previously established bijection to conclude the proof
  exact h

  have h_main : Set A ∼ 𝒫 A := by
    have h₁ : 𝒫 A ≃ Set A := by
      -- Use the existing equivalence between the powerset of A and the set of subsets of the subtype A
      apply Set.powersetEquivSetSubtype

    exact h₁.equinumerous.symm
  exact h_main
